extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true ONLY by r1, which requires all ten of: pregnancy, bradycardia, low_ejection_fraction, ckd_dialysis, immunosuppressed, seizure_history, elderly, hepatic_impairment, diabetes, peptic_ulcer. No other rule writes rec_drug_a, so rec_drug_a=true implies exactly that full ten-condition conjunction holds (and otherwise defaults to false). In precisely that configuration, two rules target rec_drug_b: r3 (priority 50) IF pregnancy THEN rec_drug_b:=true matches, and r4 (priority 90) IF (same ten-condition conjunction) THEN rec_drug_b:=false also matches. Conflict resolution is by highest priority per output; r4's priority 90 dominates r3's priority 50, so rec_drug_b is forced false whenever the ten conditions hold. Therefore whenever rec_drug_a=true, rec_drug_b=false, and they can never both be true. The deliberate trap (r3 making rec_drug_b true under pregnancy) is overridden by the higher-priority r4 in exactly the only scenario where rec_drug_a is true. The property holds over the entire input space.
